results.question: Show annual revenue, total cost, gross profit, gross margin percentage, and total order lines by fiscal year. Return columns: Fiscal Year, total_revenue, total_cost, gross_profit, gross_margin_pct, total_order_lines. Sort by Fiscal Year ascending.
results.sql:
SELECT
    '2020' as Fiscal Year,
    SUM(CASE WHEN OrderDate > '2020-01-01' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as Total Revenue,
    SUM(CASE WHEN OrderDate > '2020-01-01' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as Total Cost,
    SUM(CASE WHEN OrderDate > '2020-01-01' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as Gross Profit,
    SUM(CASE WHEN OrderDate > '2020-01-01' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as Gross Margin %
FROM Customer

results.question: Show order lines, revenue, units sold, revenue per unit (total revenue ÷ total units sold), average list price per product in the subcategory, gross profit, and margin percentage for each product subcategory. Return columns: Subcategory, order_lines, revenue, units_sold, revenue_per_unit, avg_list_price, gross_profit, margin_pct. Sort by revenue descending.
results.sql:
SELECT 
    Product.Standard Cost as revenue,
    Product.Standard Cost as units_sold,
    Product.Standard Cost as revenue_per_unit,
    Product.Standard Cost as avg_list_price,
    Product.Standard Cost as gross_profit,
    Product.Standard Cost as margin_pct
FROM Product
WHERE Product.Standard Cost = 0
GROUP BY Product.Standard Cost
ORDER BY revenue DESC

results.question: Show year-over-year revenue growth by channel and product category, comparing consecutive fiscal years. Return columns: Channel, Category, prior_year, prior_revenue, current_year, current_revenue, yoy_growth_pct. Sort by Channel, Category, then prior_year all ascending.

Show the number of products, average list price, minimum list price, and maximum list price for each product category. Return columns: Category, product_count, avg_list_price, min_list_price, max_list_price. Sort by product_count descending.

Show the number of resellers, number of distinct business types, and number of distinct states/provinces for each country. Return columns: Country-Region, reseller_count, business_type_count, state_count. Sort by reseller_count descending.
results.sql:
SELECT 
  COUNT(*) as reseller_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT Business Type) as business_type_count,
  COUNT(DISTINCT State-Province) as state_count
FROM Reseller
